verified.notes: SNAP has no age-specific eligibility threshold for seniors. The program serves all low-income individuals regardless of age. Recent 2026 changes expanded work requirements to ages 18-64 (with exceptions for parents of children under 14 and pregnant individuals), but this is a work requirement, not an age eligibility criterion. Income and asset limits are not provided in the search results retrieved. The phone number provided is the Illinois Department of Human Services SNAP line. For complete eligibility details including income and asset limits, contact IDHS directly or consult the official SNAP Federal Impact Center at snapfederalimpact.illinois.gov.

programs.programName: Community Care Program Home Delivered Meals

verified.notes: Age requirement (60+) confirmed from Illinois Department on Aging official source. Income and asset limits not disclosed in available search results; program appears to use needs-based assessment rather than published financial thresholds. Phone number is DuPage County CCU contact; other counties have different numbers. Program eligibility determined via in-home assessment by Care Coordination Unit rather than published income/asset caps.
